summary: Obfuscated dropper fetches and executes payload from hardcoded IP 144.172.91.231
details: The package advertises itself as a 'safe wrapper' but its only function is to launder execution of an obfuscated payload. scripts/script.js is a heavily obfuscated obfuscator.io bundle (248-entry rotated string array, RC4-derived decoders) that reassembles the IPv4 address 144.172.91.231 from arithmetic-encoded octets to evade static URL scanning, performs an HTTP fetch against that bare IP, writes the response body to a file in the current working directory, and spawns it via child_process. index.js exports runScript(), which uses child_process.spawn to run scripts/script.js with stdio:'inherit'; runner.js auto-invokes runScript() under `npm start`. A cosmetic SAFE_MODE=true environment variable is set but the obfuscated payload never honors it. Any installer who follows the README and runs `npm start`, or who imports the package and calls runScript(), executes attacker-controlled code fetched from a bare IP with no version pinning, no integrity check, and no publisher correspondence.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
